titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Norman Weissman Papers
titlestmt.author: by Nora Soto

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday January 26, 2024 at 04:25

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection contains correspondence, writings, research materials, photographs, printed material, and computer and audiovisual media by or relating to writer and director Norman Weissman.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The collection contains correspondence, writings, research materials, photographs, printed material, and computer and audiovisual media by or relating to writer and director Norman Weissman. Correspondence consists of personal and professional letters exchanged between Weissman and other writers and educators such as Louis Paul Bénézet and Richard L. Ottinger, as well as third party correspondence between Weissman's family members. Writings contain notes, research materials, proposals, and drafts of Weissman's books, play scripts, and screenplays. Much of Weissman's work focuses on the prevention of terrorism, the Kent State shootings, and the Vietnam War.

bioghist.head: Norman Weissman (1925–2021)
bioghist.p: Norman Weissman was an American writer, director, and producer of over 400 documentary and educational films and television specials. After graduating from Dartmouth College in 1948, Weissman began creating films for government agencies, including the United States Information Agency, the Navy, Air Force, and Department of Labor, as well as corporations such as Pfizer, Georgia Pacific, IBM, and Shell Oil, and non-profit groups like the National Heart Association and the Cerebral Palsy Foundation. Weissman directed his first film, An Adventure in Friendship , in 1949. His other films include Here to There (1965), which was shown during the 1965 New York World's Fair, Impulse '90 (1969), It Happened in Holland (1970), and Everybody Knows (1958), for which he wrote the story and screenplay. Weissman's 1953 film The School of the Ozarks was created for dissemination by the United States Information Agency to Europe during the Cold War. Weissman's honors include three American Film Festival Awards, a CHRIS Award, and an Academy Award nomination. In addition to his career in film and television production, Weissman authored six books, including the memoir My Exuberant Voyage (2009).

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five series: I. Correspondence, 1942–1949. II. Writings and Research Material, 1948–2014. III. Photographs, circa 1945–2009. IV. Printed Material, 1943–2014. V. Computer and Audiovisual Media, undated.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 14 (electronic media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 15-16 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Norman Weissman Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Norman Weissman Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Printed material received with the collection was removed for separate cataloging and can be accessed by searching the library's online catalog.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains Weissman's correspondence with writers, educators, friends, and family. Correspondents include educator Louis Paul Bénézet and politician Richard L. Ottinger. Also present is third party correspondence, including letters exchanged between Weissman's family members.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Norman Weissman Correspondence, and Third Party Correspondence. Each subseries is arranged alphabetically by correspondent, with unsorted correspondence filed at the end of the sequence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of notes, drafts, shooting scripts, project proposals, and research materials pertaining to Weissman's books, films, and television specials. Common research topics include terrorism, the Vietnam War, the Kent State shootings, and Israel.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: The Cry of Vietnam , poetry by Thich Nhat Hanh, drawings by Vo-Dinh
Israel's Strategic Doctrine , Yoav Ben-Horin and Barry Posen
"Brooklyn is My Paris," original television play
"The Holocaust," Martyrs' and Heroes' Remembrance Authority, Yad Vashem, Jerusalem
"Recognition and Management of Depression," complete uncredited transcript
In memory of Joe Arnold and Fred Flach
"Choices: A Professional Approach to Quality Assurance"
Proposal for television series on Black heritage in American history
"Through White Eyes" and "Portraits of the Vanishing West"
"Development of a Medical Education Curriculum for the Olympus Satellite"
"Everybody Knows," scenario of a sound picture in color
Petition to President Nixon to declare Dr. Samuel A. Mudd innocent
Lt. David Trujillo, My Lai witness
"Les imposteurs," Roman par Max M. Rainat
"Development of Immunological Capacity in the Newborn" and "Norpax," shooting scripts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series contains photographic prints and negatives used in Weissman's productions, as well as images promoting his books. Also included are photographic reproductions mounted on foam board.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: Photographs of Norman Weissman in the Navy
Abraham and Isaac, Kent State Memorial
My Exuberant Voyage , book jacket photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains clippings, circulars, government publications, reports, and magazines, many of which were utilized by Weissman in his work as a filmmaker. The majority of material is related to research topics such as terrorism, the military, and the Arab-Israeli conflict. There are publications from the United States Department of State, the Bureau of Diplomatic Security, the Overseas Security Advisory Council, and the Department of the Army.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: New York Times , "A Reply to Miss Ozick"
Newspaper clippings on the Holocaust, Nazism, WWII
Newspaper clippings on Palestine and Israel

title.part: Security Guidelines for American Enterprises Abroad
Private Sector Liaison Staff Anniversary Date List
Americans Abroad: What You Should Know Before You Go!
Significant Incidents of Political Violence Against Americans
Bureau of Diplomatic Security 1987 Progress Report
Security Guidelines for American Families Living Abroad
Guidelines for Protecting U.S. Business Information Overseas
Survey of the Counterintelligence Needs of Private Industry

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters to Norman Weissman from Overseas Security Advisory Council (August 1995).

title.part: Terrorist Attacks on US Businesses Abroad
The Web of Hate: Extremists Exploit the Internet

did.unittitle: "Aerial Tactics of World War I: A Brief and Somewhat Factual Historical Presentation" by Greg Keshishian
U.S. Army Command and General Staff College, Terrorism Counteraction
United States Army Military Police School
United States Department of the Army

title.part: Countering Terrorism and Other Major Disruptions on Military Installations
Countering Terrorism on US Army Installations
US Army Operational Concept for Terrorism Counteraction

did.unittitle: United States Department of Defense, Protection of DoD Personnel Against Terrorist Acts
Naval Air Primary Training Command, Wartime Military Courtesy
International Association of Chiefs of Police, Clandestine Tactics and Technology
The Mark III Slipstream Yearbook, Aviation Cadet Regiment U.S. Naval Air Training

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains compact discs, audiocassettes, microcassettes, and videocassettes.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Computer Media, and Audiovisual Media. Audiovisual Media is arranged alphabetically by format.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 14 (electronic media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 15-16 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
